request.question: What was Debbie Rowe's husband dubbed?

title: Jackson v. Jackson
Lin Huiyin
Michael Jackson
Debbie Rowe
Debbie Purdy
Buy Me
Snarling Beasties
Gallery of Fear
List of The Millers episodes
Paris Jackson (actress)
sentences: Deborah Rowe–Jackson v. Michael Joseph Jackson settled the divorce of Michael Jackson and his second wife Debbie Rowe.
Full custody was granted to Jackson.
Rowe is allowed visitation rights.
She gets property in Palm Beach, Van Nuys and Miami.
Rowe alleged sexual abuse as mentioned in the last amendment in the divorce.
Lin Huiyin (; known as Phyllis Lin or Lin Whei-yin when in the United States; 10 June 1904 – 1 April 1955) was a noted 20th-century Chinese architect and writer.

She is known to be the first female architect in modern China and her husband the famed "Father of Modern Chinese Architecture" Liang Sicheng, both of whom worked as founders and faculty in the newly formed Architecture Department of Northeastern University in 1928 and, after 1949, as professors in Tsinghua University in Beijing.
Liang and Lin began restoration work on cultural heritage sites of China in the post-imperial Republican Era of China; a passion which she would pursue to the end of her life.
The American artist Maya Lin is her niece.
<ref name = "Rowe/Kuan">Peter G. Rowe, Seng Kuan, "Architectural Encounters With Essence and Form in Modern China", MIT Press, 2002, p.219, ISBN  </ref>
Michael Joseph Jackson (August 29, 1958 – June 25, 2009) was an American singer, songwriter, and dancer.
Dubbed the "King of Pop", he was one of the most popular entertainers in the world, and was the best-selling music artist at the time of his death.
Jackson's contributions to music, dance, and fashion along with his publicized personal life made him a global figure in popular culture for over four decades.
Deborah Jeanne Rowe (born December 6, 1958) is an American nurse known for her marriage to Michael Jackson, with whom she had two children.
She lives in Palmdale, California.

Debbie Purdy (4 May 1963 – 23 December 2014) was a British music journalist and political activist from Bradford, West Yorkshire, with primary progressive multiple sclerosis, notable for her challenge to the law in England and Wales as relates to assisted suicide.
On 20 September 2009, it was announced that guidelines on assisted suicide law would be published by the UK Government.

The guidelines for England and Wales "come after a legal battle won by Debbie Purdy", as "Law Lords accepted earlier this year that [Purdy] had a right to know whether her husband would be prosecuted if he helped her to travel abroad to commit suicide."
Buy Me is a television program that has aired on HGTV in the U.S. since 2005, and on HGTV Canada since 2003, where it is that cable network's most popular show.
It is also seen in Belgium and South Africa, either dubbed or with subtitles.
It is produced by Whalley-Abbey Media Holdings (WHAM), which is owned by Debbie Travis and her husband, and produced "Debbie Travis’ Facelift".
The Snarling Beasties were formed in 1986 by Debbie Isitt and Mark Kilmurry and were influenced by the theatre of Steven Berkoff.
After performing "East" by Berkoff, Isitt became the theatre company's writer and director.
Mark Kilmurry played the lead parts in her first plays, "Gangsters" (1988), "Punch & Judy", (1989), "Femme Fatal" (1990) and "The Woman Who Cooked Her Husband" (1992).
The theatre company was very successful and won many awards including the Time Out theatre award, The Perrier Pick of the Fringe (three times) and The Independent Theatre Award.
Their theatre mixed a cinematic style with total theatre and created physical, dynamic and naturalistic plays.
The company was very influential but disbanded in 1996 though Debbie and Mark are both very successful in their chosen creative roles and still plan to do more work in the future.
Gallery of Fear (2013) is a four-part horror anthology, directed by Alan Rowe Kelly and Anthony G. Summer.
Kelly also wrote the screenplay for the film based on a story by Doug Smith.
The film stars Debbie Rochon, Raine Brown, and Susan Adriensen.
The four segments of the anthology are titled "Critics Choice," "By Her Hand, She Draws You Down," "Down the Drain," and "A Far Cry from Home."
The film was distributed by Southpaw Pictures and Tiny Core Pictures.
"The Millers" is an American sitcom that was created by Greg Garcia.
The multi-camera series premiered on CBS as part of the 2013–14 American television season and aired from October 3, 2013 to July 18, 2015.
"The Millers" is set in Leesburg, Virginia where Nathan Miller is a local television news reporter.
His sister Debbie runs a combination yoga studio/vegan restaurant with her husband Adam, with whom she has a daughter named Mikayla.
Nathan does not have any children and often hangs out with Ray, his friend who also serves as a cameraman for the fictional television station at which Nathan works.
As the series begins, Nathan and Debbie's bickering parents, Tom and Carol Miller, are returning from Myrtle Beach.
Nathan informs them he divorced his wife, Janice, several months ago.
Tom responds by deciding to leave Carol after being married for forty-three years.
Tom moves in with Debbie and Adam while Carol moves in with Nathan, each driving their children crazy.
On March 13, 2014, "The Millers" was renewed for a second season, which premiered on October 20, 2014.
Paris-Michael Katherine Jackson (born April 3, 1998) is an American actress, activist, and model.
She is the second child and only daughter of Michael Jackson and Debbie Rowe.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include both entity tokens from the inputs and a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the evidence bridge to retrieve (e.g., "EntityA EntityB director").
- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single text field only):
- Output exactly one concise summary string (no extra fields, labels, or metadata). Keep the summary to 1–2 short sentences unless the evidence forces a single-token identification (see below).
- Primary goal: integrate the first-hop identity in `context` with facts found in `passages` so the final answer agent has the explicit, supported linkage it needs.

When the question requires linking the context entity to information in the passages and the passages supply the bridging fact:
- Produce a concise (1–2 sentence) integrated summary that (1) names the context entity (using the form present in `context`), (2) names the supporting entity/attribute found in `passages` (using the form present in the inputs), and (3) states the explicit relation or fact (membership, creator attribution, partnership, founding/establishment, award, etc.) exactly as supported by the inputs. Use only facts present in `context` and `passages` and avoid introducing any unsupported details.

Name and attribution formatting:
- If a canonical multi-token name (given + family name or full official name) appears in the inputs, use that full form in the summary; do not truncate to a surname or partial name.
- For questions about creator/author/credit, prefer the person-name attribution as given in the inputs over returning the title of a work or event.

Identification requests:
- If the question explicitly asks for a single identification (for example, asks "which X" or otherwise requests a named token or label), return exactly the canonical name(s) as presented in the passages. If multiple valid names are requested, return them comma-separated. Do not add explanatory clauses, relational fragments, or extra text in these cases—only the canonical name(s).

When evidence is missing or ambiguous:
- If the passages do not supply a clear bridging fact, state that succinctly (e.g., "No supporting evidence in the provided passages" or "Unclear from the provided sources"). Retain uncertainty rather than asserting unsupported facts.

Conciseness and fidelity constraints:
- Keep the summary short and factual (1–2 sentences). Do not expand into long biographies, exhaustive lists, or unrelated details.
- Do not assert facts that are not present in `context` and `passages`.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passage text, but short verbatim names or phrases necessary for fidelity are allowed.
- Treat the `context` field as the primary first-hop identity to be linked to `passages` when integrating evidence.

Produce the `summary` now based only on the supplied `question`, `context`, and `passages`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use summary_1 and summary_2 as the sole evidential basis for the final answer; give greatest weight to summary_2 (the later, post-retrieval integrated evidence). Do not introduce facts from outside these summaries.

Output rules (apply in order):

1) If summary_1 and summary_2 clearly and unambiguously agree on the same concise answer, set the value of `answer` to exactly one minimal string and nothing else:
   - For a binary yes/no conclusion, emit the single lowercase token: yes or no.
   - For any other concise label, emit that label exactly as it appears in the summaries (trim leading/trailing whitespace and strip any surrounding punctuation or wrapper syntax such as surrounding parentheses, quotes, or JSON-like braces). Do not add words, sentences, punctuation, quotes, brackets, or any other surrounding text.

2) Preserve multi-token labels: if any visible summary contains a multi-token form of the answer (for example a full personal name, a multi-word company or place), output that full multi-token form verbatim. Only shorten a label if no multi-token form appears in either summary.

3) Company/developer preference: when the question requests a company or developer and the summaries show a subordinate studio together with an explicit parent/publisher label (e.g., in parentheses or with phrasing like "published by"), choose the parent/publisher name as the single minimal answer string. If no parent/publisher is explicitly indicated, choose the best-supported developer/studio name from the summaries.

4) If the summaries conflict or are ambiguous, do NOT apply the unanimous-agreement rule in (1). Instead, use summary_2 as the primary basis and emit the minimal concise string supported by summary_2, applying rules (2) and (3) as relevant. If summary_2 provides no concise supported answer, set `answer` to the minimal explicit token "unknown".

Formatting constraints (must always be followed):
- Produce exactly one field: `answer`.
- The value of `answer` must be a single minimal string (no surrounding text, punctuation, explanation, or structural wrappers).
- Do not output extra fields, labels, or markup.

Additional guidance:
- If summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge but summary_2 provides a resolved, supported conclusion, use the conclusion from summary_2.
- If a concise answer string can be drawn verbatim from the summaries, prefer that verbatim text over paraphrase.

End.

response.hop1_query: What was Debbie Rowe's husband dubbed?
hop1_docs: Buy Me | Buy Me is a television program that has aired on HGTV in the U.S. since 2005, and on HGTV Canada since 2003, where it is that cable network's most popular show.  It is also seen in Belgium and South Africa, either dubbed or with subtitles.  It is produced by Whalley-Abbey Media Holdings (WHAM), which is owned by Debbie Travis and her husband, and produced "Debbie Travis’ Facelift".

Debbie Burton | Debbie Burton was an American singer.  She is best known for dubbing the singing voice of the young Baby Jane Hudson (played by child actress Julie Allred) in the 1962 film "What Ever Happened to Baby Jane? ", singing the song "I've Written a Letter to Daddy".  Burton also sang a duet with Bette Davis, the rock and roll song "What Ever Happened to Baby Jane?" , written by Frank DeVol and Lukas Heller.  It was released as a promotional single, with Burton's rendition of "I've Written a Letter to Daddy" on the flipside.  An instrumental version of "What Ever Happened to Baby Jane?"  can be heard in the movie.

Betty Noyes | Elizabeth Noyes Hand (October 11, 1912 – December 24, 1987) was a singer and actress best known for dubbing two of Debbie Reynolds' numbers in the 1952 film "Singin' in the Rain."  Today, this is a well-known example of dubbing in a movie musical; ironically, Reynolds's character in "Singin' in the Rain" was supposedly dubbing for another character.

Jackson v. Jackson | Deborah Rowe–Jackson v. Michael Joseph Jackson settled the divorce of Michael Jackson and his second wife Debbie Rowe.  Full custody was granted to Jackson.  Rowe is allowed visitation rights.  She gets property in Palm Beach, Van Nuys and Miami.  Rowe alleged sexual abuse as mentioned in the last amendment in the divorce.
Debbie Rowe | Deborah Jeanne Rowe (born December 6, 1958) is an American nurse known for her marriage to Michael Jackson, with whom she had two children.  She lives in Palmdale, California.

Debbie Boyd | Debbie Boyd (born November, 1957 in Gainesville, Florida) is a Representative in the Florida House of Representatives.  She received her associate degree in building construction from Santa Fe Community College in 1990.  She resides in Newberry, Florida and lives with her husband Tommy Boyd.

Debbi Fields | Debbi Fields (born September 18, 1956) is the founder and spokesperson of Mrs. Fields Bakeries.  Additionally, she has written several cookbooks and makes cookies.  She currently lives in Nashville, Tennessee, with her husband, former Holiday Inn and Harrah's CEO Michael Rose.
response.summary_1:
Evidence found:
- Debbie Rowe — [5]: "Deborah Jeanne Rowe (born December 6, 1958)"
- Debbie Rowe known relation — [5]: "known for her marriage to Michael Jackson"
- Michael Joseph Jackson — [4]: "Michael Joseph Jackson"
- Michael Jackson (divorce mention) — [4]: "the divorce of Michael Jackson and his second wife Debbie Rowe"

Ambiguities:
- "Michael Joseph Jackson" vs "Michael Jackson" (passages [4], [5]) — same person label variants.
- "husband" could ambiguously refer to Debbie Travis's husband in [1] or Debbie Rowe's husband in [4],[5]; question specifies Debbie Rowe so target is Michael Jackson.
- Possible nickname variants not shown in passages: e.g., "King of Pop" vs "the King of Pop" (not present).

Bridge / missing fact:
- The epithet/nickname that Michael Jackson (Debbie Rowe's husband) was "dubbed" (the nickname itself, e.g., "King of Pop") is not stated in the provided passages.

Retrieval direction:
- Open page: "Michael Jackson"
- Open page: "King of Pop"
- Search query: "\"Michael Jackson\" dubbed \"King of Pop\""
- Search query: "What was Michael Jackson dubbed"
Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets above.
hop2_docs: Jackson v. Jackson | Deborah Rowe–Jackson v. Michael Joseph Jackson settled the divorce of Michael Jackson and his second wife Debbie Rowe.  Full custody was granted to Jackson.  Rowe is allowed visitation rights.  She gets property in Palm Beach, Van Nuys and Miami.  Rowe alleged sexual abuse as mentioned in the last amendment in the divorce.

Paris Jackson (actress) | Paris-Michael Katherine Jackson (born April 3, 1998) is an American actress, activist, and model.  She is the second child and only daughter of Michael Jackson and Debbie Rowe.
Debbie Rowe | Deborah Jeanne Rowe (born December 6, 1958) is an American nurse known for her marriage to Michael Jackson, with whom she had two children.  She lives in Palmdale, California.

The Original Soul of Michael Jackson | The Original Soul of Michael Jackson is a remix album of songs by Michael Jackson, recorded and published early in his solo career in the early 1970s and remixed in 1987.  While the album claims to 'contains music never before released', the only new song available was a re-dubbed cover of Edwin Starr's 1970 hit "Twenty-Five Miles", sung by the Jackson Five and solely credited to Michael.  The original recording of the song was included on the 2009 set "".  Some songs were newly edited on the album, with a newly dubbed version of "Dancing Machine". " Ain't No Sunshine" were also slightly different in the vocals and a modern drum machine were added to "Twenty-Five Miles".  "Melodie" was planned for a single in an effort to promote the record in the US, but was scrapped for a promo-only single "Twenty-Five Miles"/"Up On The Housetop".

Michael Jackson | Michael Joseph Jackson (August 29, 1958 – June 25, 2009) was an American singer, songwriter, and dancer.  Dubbed the "King of Pop", he was one of the most popular entertainers in the world, and was the best-selling music artist at the time of his death.  Jackson's contributions to music, dance, and fashion along with his publicized personal life made him a global figure in popular culture for over four decades.

2009 MTV Video Music Awards | The 2009 MTV Video Music Awards took place on September 13, 2009, at Radio City Music Hall in New York City, honoring the best music videos from June 19, 2008, to June 29, 2009.  Comedian Russell Brand hosted the event for the second time in a row.  The awards were held a few months after Michael Jackson died.  The VMAs dedicated the night to Jackson with an opening performance featuring a music video montage medley with dancers performing Jackson's choreography and concluding with a special appearance by Janet Jackson performing her duet "Scream" alongside footage of her brother.  Madonna opened the show with a speech about Jackson.  For the finale of the show, they showed the trailer of "Michael Jackson's This Is It".

Michael Dansby | Michael Dansby (born June 30, 1983) is a former American football offensive linemen.  He attended Jackson State.  In 2007, Michael made his pro debut for the Arkansas Twisters of the af2 under head coach John Gregory.  In 2008 Dansby signed with the Iowa Barnstormers.  He has been named the Barnstormers "Linemen of the Year" for three years in a row (2008-2010).
hop2_titles: The Original Soul of Michael Jackson
response.summary_2: Debbie Rowe's husband, Michael Joseph Jackson, was dubbed the "King of Pop."
